title: Tania Niwa - Wooden MƒÅori Culture Puzzles - Set of
main description: A set of three MƒÅori puzzles. These photographic puzzles feature three iconic images from New Zealand. These area Hongi performed by a school boy and a school girl, four school childrenperforming Kapa haka, and a respected Kaumatua.  Each wooden puzzle measures 20 x 30cm and has 24 pieces.These MƒÅori puzzles feature beautiful photographs which are printed directly on to the wooden puzzle. This ensures the puzzles are durable and long lasting, and the printed photographs will not peel off from the puzzle's surface.  These wooden MƒÅori puzzles are suitable for use in an early childhood environment as well as a lower primary school classroom.  The photograph was taken by Tania Niwa.  New ZealandphotographerTania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She isan internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her MƒÅori heritage has been the source of an ongoing ss of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties MƒÅori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand

title: Nga Pihi 3 - MƒÅori Songboo
main description: This MƒÅori songbook accompanies the Nga Pihi 3 CD (OD46CD, sold separately). The MƒÅori songbook contains teaching notes, lyrics, translations, chords and melody lines for all songs. Aunty Tawai Te Ranga offers putake for each waiata. title: MƒÅori Language Blocks - 24 piece
main description: This set of 24 wooden embossed blocks feature the Te Reo alphabet. These MƒÅori blocks also include maths symbols, traditional carving patterns, and figures from six iwi throughout New Zealand. The figures of Tne, Tangaroa, Twhiri-mtea, T…matauenga, Haumia-tiketike and Rongo-m-Tne can be stacked on top of one another and are interchanged with tukutuku (ornamental lattice) patterns and letters.  These MƒÅori blocks are designed by Wellington artist Johnson Witehira, the blocks are made from basswood. Each block measures 4.5cm. Suitable for children from the age of 2 years. This resource helps children to understand the people and the world around them.  Our range of global and multicultural resources help to support children's learning about their communities, as well as the world in general. These resources aid in the promotion of understanding and acceptance of different cultures, beliefs, languages, families, appearances and lifestyles. As children gain an understanding of their community, they also in an understanding of themselves. This develops confidence and a sense of identity. Incorporating multicultural resources into play is a great way to introduce these concepts in fun and unobtrusive ways.  Play is an important part of early childhood. Play encourages children to learn, develop and grow whilst they have fun. Through play, children can develop important cognitive and physical skills. Play encourages creativity, imagination, perseverance and the development of language and social skills.  We love creating products that allow indigenous communities and artists to communicate their culture through their art and in turn allowing children to develop awareness and understanding of the significance of cultural heritage in their world. If you are an indigenous artist and would like to work with MTA to also develop culturally sensitive and appropriate education resources, please contact info@teaching.com.au.

title: Black Sand Beach Discovery Play Mat
main description: This beautiful playmat depicts the black sand beaches of New Zealand. The photographic print features a scene depicting black sand, volcanic rock, shells, leaves and paua shells. The play mat measures 150 x 100cm, and is made from a durable polyester, with a non-slip rubber surface on the back of the play mat. Perfect for creative and imaginative role play activities, this playmat will add a beautiful atmosphere to any classroom environment. Use for dividing play spaces or for inspiring discussions on topics such as habitats, animals, coastlines, geography, weather and patterns. The photograph was taken by Tania Niwa. New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her MƒÅori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to inorporate in the early nineties MƒÅori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand

title: Kapa Haka Kotiro Girl Dress Up
main description:
ThisKapa Haka Kotiro girl dress up costume depicts the Kiwi, and is surrounded by the Niho-Taniwha pattern (dragon teeth) and the three Baskets of Knowledge. The costume features a bodice with black underskirt and a short, stylised cotton piupiu (traditional MƒÅori flax skirt) and a tipare (head-band). Features velcro closures. Suitable for children ages 3 - 6 years.

Role play helps children to develop language and communication skills. It develops and promotes creativity and imagination. Dramatic and imaginative play also encourages children to become more confident and develop a stronger sense of identity. When children come together in role play, it aids in their social and emotional development. Listening to others, negotiation and co-operation all result from authentic dramatic play. This multicultural dress up set of costume is excellent for promoting social and imaginative play. Role play and dramatic play encourages language development, teaches daily routines and skills plus develops social interations, sharing and a sense of belonging and identity. Play is an important part of early childhood. As a result play encourages children to learn, develop and grow whilst they have fun.

These resources aid in the promotion of understanding and acceptance of different cultures, beliefs, languages, families, appearances and lifestyles. As children gain an understanding o community, they also gain an understanding of themselves. This develops confidence and a sense of identity.  Incorporating multicultural resources into play is a great way to introduce these concepts in fun and unobtrusive ways. Play is an important part of early childhood. Play encourages children to learn, develop and grow whilst they have fun. Through play, children can develop important cognitive and physical skills. Play encourages creativity, imagination, perseverance and the development of language and social skills.

title: Striped Teepee and Discovery Playmat Kit
main description: A wonderful space for play and imagination! This kit includes Striped Teepee (ZH001) and Black Sand Beach Discovery Play Mat (SPP56). The contents of this kit may vary. Children will love playing in this colourful tepee tent. It features a window with cover, front flaps and a floor sheet. Easy to assemble, this teepee will invite exploration and play.The tee pee tent measures 170w x 155cm high. Use with supervision and do not leave outdoors. This beautiful play mat depicts the black sand beaches of New Zealand. The photographic print features a scene depicting black sand, volcanic rock, shells, leaves and paua shells. The play mat measures 150 x 100cm, and is made from a durable polyester, with a non-slip rubber surface on the back of the play mat. Perfect for creative and imaginative role play activities, this play mat will add a beautiful atmosphere to any classroom environment. Use for dividing play spaces or for inspiring discussions on topics such as habitats, animals, coastlines, geography, weather and patterns. The photograph was taken by Tania Niwa. New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her MƒÅori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties MƒÅori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealan

title: English-MƒÅori Dictionary Sof
main description: Pukapuka Tikanga Kupu English-Māori Dictionary by H.M. Ngata is the most comprehensive English-Māori dictionary available, offering over 14,500 headwords. It is the first major dictionary to be written by MƒÅori for the study of te reo Māori. The work was originated more than thirty years ago by Hōri Ngata, and has been compiled by native speakers of the language, including many Ngāti Porou kaumtua, with the help of a team of linguists and editors. The dictionary has a simple format, providing headwords in English and Māori, with sentences illustrating usage. Commentary and grammatical material have been kept to a minimum, a simply set-out guide illustrates how to use the dictionary. Appendices list terms for flora and fauna, points of the compass, major New Zealand towns, the terminology of kinship and categories of possessive relationships. You can also get free access to the electronic Ngata Dictionary in both English-Māori and Māori-English at www.learningmedia.co.nz

title: Nest Of Singing Birds
main description:
Nest Of Singing Birds celebrates one hundred years of the New Zealand School Journal. A Nest of Singing Birds celebrates, in words and images, the publication that has shaped in some way New Zealand literature. Over the course of its history The School Journal has attracted works from some of New Zealand's greatest poets, painters and writers, among them Margaret Mahy, James K. Baxter, Janet Frame, Rita Angus, Russell Clark, and Dick Frizzell.Started in 1907, and surviving the educational cuts of the 1950s, the New Zealand School Journal has remained part of New Zealand's educational scene. Produced quarterly by the Schools Publication Branch of the Department of Education, the School Journal was pitched at primary school children, who then went on to read the Post-Primary School Bulletin.
The Schools Publication Branch has since become Learning Media, and this restructured organization has now sponsored a centennial publication dedicated to its prized publication. At least some things do not change: whatever the alterations to its style and content, the School Journal remains the School Journal. Rather than change, it has constantly adapted, in recent years to "accommodate more of the young person's world and [End Page 240] world view"skateboarding is depicted but the skaters are wearing helmets and safety gear. Adaptations have also been made for the School Journal to withstand the challenges that television and computer games pose to a culture of reading among children."A Nest of Singing Birds" is how the School Journal's office was described to the poet Alistair Campbell, who himself contributed to, and at one point edited the Journal. A particular strength of the book under review is its lavish illustration with images from the Journals themselves. These commissions for the School Journal meant that the country's artists and wood engravers got much needed work and income. Writers and poets also found an outlet in the School Journal, and in many cases their work for the Journal was the foundation of a career. I know from growing up in New Zealand in the 1950s and 1960s how difficult an environment it was for the arts generally (my parents were musicians), and one wonders what the country's cultural heritage would now be but for impetus provided by the School Journal. Some of this heritage is there to behold in this tastefully-designed, beautifully-presented and amply-proportioned Nest Of Singing Birds book.
Gregory O'Brien's lively text traces the development of the School Journal and does not shirk at recounting "conflict and occasional controversy" as the Journal adapted to changing circumstances or when restrained from time to time by societal disapproval. The interplay of personalities and the dynamics of decision-making are particularly well handled, and his discussion of the illustrations is nothing if not well-informed. All the same, and while accepting that O'Brien has avowedly emphasized the contributing artists and writers, certain themes warrant further attention. There is attenuated discussion on how the School Journal may have influenced its readersthat is to say, how it might have imparted a broader vision of New Zealand and the world, and inculcated "acceptable" attitudes toward country and Empire [pp. 148-52]and no references to the secondary literature in the field.1 Second, there is scope for discussion on relating the School Journal's content to existing school syllabi. Crucially, the School Journal was far more concerned with local than overseas content. Contrast this, say, with what the British educational authorities were doing in the 1950s for primary school children in Britain's remaining colonies. School text books in Fiji during the 1950s and 1960s exclusively focused on overseas subjects and experiences.2 There is nothing wrong with gaining, through books, a little of the wisdom that Ulysses attained through knowing many places and other people's manners, but not to the exclusion of one's own country or locality. Whatever one might say about the ethnocentricity of some of the School Journal's content, or the stylized and romantic depiction of the MƒÅori people, it cannot be said that it screened out the local. In this regard it mght have been useful had O'Brien interviewed people for their memories of the School Journal and how it might have influenced them.These caveats apart, A Nest of Singing Birds is a remarkable publication that successfully integrates text and image and which deserves a readership well beyond New...Learning Media believes that literacy is at the heart of learning. Our carefully levelled print and digital texts for guided, shared and independent reading will support and challenge your students.
Backed by thorough research and built on current and effective pedagogy, Learning Media's literacy learning resources help meet the learning and teaching needs of every student and teacher. Learning Media has been a leading education resource supplier for schools in Australian and New Zealand for over 20 years. We bring high-quality, innovative literacy resources to the education community - resources centred on evidence-based instruction and designed with the knowledge of how teachers teach and students learn.

title: Raranga Outdoor Recycled Rectangular Floor Mat - 180 x 300cm
main description: This environmentally friendly outdoor mat is made from recycled plastic. The recycled mat measures 300cm x 180cm. The mat is fade resistant and can be easily hosed for cleaning. As it is made from recycled plastic, this floor mat is an environmentally friendly and sustainable choice. This colourful, floor mat features a Raranga weaving border. The woven links represent unity and togetherness. The middle of the environmentally friendly mat symbolises Taniko, a traditional type of MƒÅori weaving. This recycled mat may slip on hard flooring without a non slip mat underneath. We recommend using a non slip mat under these floor mats when used on hard surfaces. Please see our website for more information on the non slip floor mats (PL204 and PL205). We love creating products that allow indigenous communities and artists to communicate their culture through their art and in turn allowing children to develop awareness and understanding of the significance of cultural heritage in their world. If you are an indigenous artist and would like to work with MTA to also develop culturally sensitive and appropriate education resources, please contact info@teaching.com.au. As per all items left outside this item may heat up in direct sunlight or in hot weather. This item is fade resistant but not fade proof. Repeated exposure to direct sunlight may result in some colour fading. We recommend to use this items in shade or filtered light and for best results do not leave in direct sunlight for long periods of time.

title: Felt Stars - Pack of 100
main description: Assorted coloured felt stars, use them to add texture to your creative classroom activities.  These stars are perfect  These stars are great for children to pin around the room, they can pretend to be under the night sky.  Children can make mobiles with the stars by gluing them to cardboard, then attaching with string or ribbon to 2 pop sticks glued in a "X" shape.  The stars are perfect for children to use during Matariki. Children can create lovely star constellations to celebrate the MƒÅori New Year which is held late May early June.  Children can paint wonderful backgrounds then glue the stars on top. This gives the piece a lovely three dimensional texture to the art work.  The stars can be hung in 3D dioramas or used in puppet shows.  Size: 2 to 6cm

title: Make a Poi - Craft Activity
main description:
Make a Poi - Craft Activity

Get the children learning about New Zealand culture by making this poi they put together and then play with. These will help the children learn about MƒÅori culture in a practical and fun way and the children can use what they have created in their own poi performance. The children will be using skills such ascutting, gluing, sewing and tying. These will bring the children together as they work as a class to make their poi's that they can use in a performance and take home to share with their families. These combine a number of different steps to help the children developtheir craft skills to produce a poi they will be proud of and can use over and over. Makes Approx. 30 pois.

title: Weaving Pattern Floor Mat & Non-Slip Mat
main description: Environmentally friendly MƒÅori floor mat is suitable for outdoors and is made from recycled plastic. The recycled mat measures 180cm x 180cm. The mat is fade resistant and can be easily hosed for cleaning. As it is made from recycled plastic, this floor mat is an environmentally friendly and sustainable choice. This colourful, MƒÅori floor mat features a curl motif, which represents the young leaf of a fern. Around the border, the pattern represents Taniko, which is a traditional weaving technique. The kit also includes a rubber non-slip floor mat. This allows for the recycled floor mat to be used on hard surfaces such as wooden floorboards and concrete flooring without the map slipping. The contents of this kit may vary. For more information see the kit components tab on the website. Through play, children are able to understand the people and the world around them. Our range of global and multicultural resources help to support children's learning about their communities, as well as the world in general. Tse resources aid in the promotion of understanding and acceptance of different cultures, beliefs, languages, families, appearances and lifestyles. As children gain an understanding of their community, they also gain an understanding of themselves. This develops confidence and a sense of identity. Incorporating multicultural resources into play is a great way to introduce these concepts in fun and unobtrusive ways. Play is an important part of early childhood. Play encourages children to learn, develop and grow whilst they have fun. Through play, children can develop important cognitive and physical skills. Play encourages creativity, imagination, perseverance and the development of language and social skills. We love creating products that allow indigenous communities and artists to communicate their culture through their art and in turn allowing children to develop awareness and understanding of the significance of cultural heritage in their world. If you are an indigenous artist and would like to work with MTA to also develop culturally sensitive and appropriate education resources, please contact info@teaching.com.au. As per all items left outside this item may heat up in direct sunlight or in hot weather. This item is fade resistant but not fade proof. Repeated exposure to direct sunlight may result in some colour fading. We recommend to use this items in shade or filtered light and for best results do not leave in direct sunlight for long periods of time. The non-slip is not suitable for outdoor use.

title: Discovery Backgrounds Complete Set - Set of 7
main description: These Discovery Backgrounds create a wonderful backdrop and scenery for any play space. The kit includes 7 play backgrounds; Beach (INK001), Billabong (INK002) Desert (INK003), Busy City (INK005), Under the Jetty (INK006) Rocky Beach (INK009), and Space (INK010). All of the play backgrounds are sold separately. Contents may vary.  Includes photography from Australian Geographic photographers as well as the New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa.  Australian Geographic features the work of many of Australia's top assignment and wilderness photographers and illustrators. With attention to the environment and sustainability, Australian Geographic has a genuine focus on promoting the beauty of the native plants and animals of Australia. The Australian Geographic images are a beautiful tribute to the majesty of the world around us.  New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her MƒÅori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal images for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties MƒÅori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealan

title: Discovery Play Mats - Set of 4
main description: These discovery play mats are excellent for any classroom environment. This kit features four individual play mats. The kit includes Red Sand (SPP53), Forest Floor (SPP60), Creek Bed (SPP52) and Black Sand (SPP56) Discovery Play Mats. These playmats feature beautiful photographs taken from the Australian Geographic photo library and from Tania Niwa. These photographs illustrate gorgeous scenes from nature and will inspire imaginative and creative role play in children. These mats are excellent for creating mini worlds and for sparking discussions on nature, the environment, animals and habitats. Each play mat is made from a durable polyester top layer and a non-slip rubberised backing, and measures 100cm wide x 150cm long. title: What's New At MTA
Tania Niwa - New Zealand Landscapes Puzzles
main description: A set of 8 New Zealand puzzles. These photographic puzzles depict the New Zealand landscape. These wooden New Zealand puzzles 8 iconic images, such as a Marae, wood carvings, beaches with volcanic rock, ferns and paua shells. A true celebration of the beautiful New Zealand landscape. These puzzles feature beautiful photographs which are digitally printed onto a laminated surface. Each puzzle is hand cut, so will vary slightly to another. Each wooden puzzle measures 20cm x 15cm and has 12 pieces. These wooden New Zealand puzzles are suitable for use in an early childhood environment as well as a lower primary school classroom. The photograph was taken by Tania Niwa. New Zealand photographer Tania Niwa is a Grand Master of Photography of the Australian Institute of Professional Photography and also a Fellow of the New Zealand Institute of Professional Photography. She is an internationally respected leader professional portrait photography. Her MƒÅori heritage has been the source of an ongoing series of tribal mages for the last two decades, and began to incorporate in the early nineties MƒÅori carvings and symbolism based on her own tribe's Marae within her subject's imagery. Tania is Australia's youngest Grand Master of Photography, and in her early career was the youngest ever to win New Zealand Professional Photographer of the Year at the age of 26 years. Her tribes are Te Atiawa and Taranaki from the west coast of the north island of New Zealand

title: Maui and Other MƒÅori Legends: 8 Classic Tales of Aotearo
main description:
A beautiful collection of artist Peter Gossage's beloved MƒÅori myths, in one stunning volume, Maui and Other MƒÅori Legends: 8 Classic Tales of Aotearoa.

Peter Gossage's memorable retellings of MƒÅori oral myths have captivated the children of New Zealand for generations. Their dramatic and distinctive illustrations with minimal yet evocative language form a powerful combination, and each has earned its place among the beloved classics of our literature.

These are exciting, magical tales of adventure and intrigue. Several feature the remarkable culture hero Maui - the quick-witted and the trickster - whose exploits include slowing the sun in its course across the sky, fishing up the North Island/Te Ika a Maui, discovering the secret of fire and his attempt to trick the goddess of death and become immortal.

Maui and Other Legends contains eight essential legends. In this volume you will find timeless favourites such as How Maui Found his Mother, Battle of the Mountains, Pania of the Reef and many more.

Threasury includes-
How Maui Found his Mother
How Maui Found his Father and the Magic Jawbone
The Fish of Maui
How Maui Slowed the Sun
How Maui Found the Secret of Fire
How Maui Defied the Goddess of Death
Battle of the Mountains
Pania of the Reef

Hardback 217 x 302mm

title: Matariki The MƒÅori New Yea
main description:
Matariki The MƒÅori New Year is an introduction to the star group Matariki. Known in other cultures by names including the Pleiades and the Seven Sisters, Matariki featured strongly in pre-European New Zealand. It marked the beginning of the MƒÅori calendar, and its rising before the sun in late May or early June was greeted with great festivals. It was used as a guide to planting and harvesting, and was studied by tohunga as an omen which told whether the food-gathering season would be plentiful or lean. Libby Hakaraia includes interviews with astronomers Richard Hall and Vicki Hyde, well known navigator Hekenukumai Busby, and Hapimana Rikihana, who still practises the ancient MƒÅori art of mahi whai, or string patterns.

title: Waiata MƒÅori Songs in Histor
main description:
In Waiata our forebears spoke their hearts - in grief and celebration. For many hundreds of years this great oral tradition of song flourished in Aotearoa. During the second half of the nineteenth century, in times of rapid change, MƒÅori scholars recorded for the future the words of thousands of waiata. Their manuscripts were preserved by Pakeha of foresight and commitment, and along with a vast body of other MƒÅori writing they are now accessible in libraries throughout the country.
Margaret Orbell has been working with these manuscripts for 25 years. She has come to occupy a special place in MƒÅori scholarship, having brought to light and translated many ancient texts. In this new anthology she places waiata of the nineteenth century in their social and political setting, conveying the poets' responses to their people's trauma.
There is a fascinating richness of detail here about traditional MƒÅori life, with insights into the lives of ordinary people as well as into tribal relations and the interaction ofri and Pakeha. Margaret also reveals the great skills of the composers - their use of imagery, rhythm and symbolism, and the profound knowledge they convey. Her authoritative and illuminating commentaries will make this collections hugely interesting to a wide range of readers.

title: Raupo Essential MƒÅori Dictionar
main description:
The Raupo Essential MƒÅori Dictionary is an invaluable introductory dictionary for students of te reo MƒÅori. It features clear, easy-to-follow MƒÅori-English and English-MƒÅori sections, with the MƒÅori and English alphabets at the top of each page, all the words a learner is likely to encounter, including contemporary usage and modern terms, a section of themed word lists, including days of the week, months of the year, numbers, cities of New Zealand, colours, emotions, actions, parts of the body, in the classroom, and on the marae.

title: Taming the Sun: Four MƒÅori Myth
main description:
Four classic MƒÅori myths beautifully retold within Taming the Sun with stunning illustrations by award winner Gavin Bishop.

Taming the Sun contains four stories, including two well-known legends (Maui And The Sun and Maui And The Big Fish) and two less well-known legends (Rona And The Moon and Kahu The Taniwha).

Aimed at children with reading ages 3-7 years, these myths are simply and yet elegantly written. Gavin's characteristic sense of humour is evident in all of the stories; and while each story is exciting and often a little bit scary, they are resolved beautifully. Gavin allows the essence of the original myths to remain while also re-telling them for a new generation of children.

The illustrations are stunning - the different colour palette for each story and the strong mix of techniques create bold and beautiful images that integrate with the words in a powerful and meaningful way. Taming the Sun is an imaginative, fresh and much-needed addition to the picture book genre, which takes traditional mths to a new level. Created by one of New Zealand's most talented and passionate children's book writers and illustrators.

SHORT-LISTED FOR NZ POST CHILDREN'S BOOK AWARDS 2005.

Gavin Bishop is a highly acclaimed children's book author and illustrator of more than 60 books, whose work ranges from original stories to retellings of MƒÅori myths, European fairy stories, and nursery rhymes.

Born in Invercargill, he spent his childhood in the remote railway settlement of Kingston on the shores of Lake Wakatipu. Studying under Russell Clark and Rudi Gopas, Gavin graduated from the Canterbury University School of Fine Arts with an honours degree in painting. He taught art at Linwood High School and at Christ's College in Christchurch.

Among the numerous fellowships and national book prizes that have been awarded to Gavin throughout his career, highlights are his Te Waka Toi Nga Tohu a Ta Kingi Ihaka/Sir Kingi Ihaka Award in 2018, recognising his lifetime contribution to strengthening MƒÅori art and culture throughis children's books; The Arts Foundation's Mallinson Rendel Illustrators Award in 2013; and the 2000 Storylines Margaret Mahy Medal for lifetime achievement and his distinguished contribution to children's literature in New Zealand. Gavin was made an Officer of the New Zealand Order of Merit in 2013, and President of Honour of the NZ Society of Authors.

title: MƒÅori Myths, Legends and Traditions Primary School Pac
main description: MƒÅori Myths, Legends and Traditions Primary School Pack introduces a plethora of rich texts which range from stories about the creation of the world, tales of adventure and intrigue, to traditional tales and MƒÅori myths and legends. This is your moment to extend your students' knowledge about MƒÅori culture. PEN5475 MƒÅori Myth and Legend -√É‚Äö√Ç¬ÝThe MƒÅori have a rich and colourful tradition of myth and legend - many of their most important and popular tales are retold in this classic, bestselling book. Written with the general reader in mind, the stories range from the creation of the world to the coming of life, death and knowledge. They incorporate the great god Tane, Maui who tamed the sun, the woman on the moon, monsters, fairies, wondrous birds and moving mountains. Born in Auckland in 1908, A.W. Reed (Alexander Wyclif) was educated at Mt Albert Grammar School. In 1932, he helped to found a book-publishing business, A.H. & A.W. Reed, one of New Zealand's leading publishing hoe time with an enduring list of local books. Reed wrote more than 200 books himself, with a range of reference works and popular accounts of MƒÅori culture. Paperback 129 x 197mm PEN9291 Maui and Other MƒÅori Legends: 8 Classic Tales of Aotearoa -√É‚Äö√Ç¬ÝA beautiful collection of artist Peter Gossage's beloved MƒÅori myths, in one stunning volume, Maui and Other MƒÅori Legends: 8 Classic Tales of Aotearoa. These are exciting, magical tales of adventure and intrigue. Several feature the remarkable culture hero Maui - the quick-witted and the trickster - whose exploits include slowing the sun in its course across the sky, fishing up the North Island/Te Ika a Maui, discovering the secret of fire and his attempt to trick the goddess of death and become immortal. The treasury includes- How Maui Found his Mother How Maui Found his Father and the Magic Jawbone The Fish of Maui How Maui Slowed the Sun How Maui Found the Secret of Fire How Maui Defied the Goddess of Death Battle of the Mountains Pan Reef Hardback 217 x 302mm PEN3330 Traditional MƒÅori Legends -√É‚Äö√Ç¬ÝTraditional MƒÅori legends. Nga Tai Korero means 'the currents of speech', a reference to the MƒÅori tradition of oral storytelling. In this colourful book, Warren Pohatu retells in simple form fourteen MƒÅori stories and myths that have been passed down over centuries - including old favourites like 'Maui and the Fish', 'Paikea and the Whale', 'Tutanekai and Hinemoa', 'Ngatoroirangi', 'Rona and the Moon', and 'Maui and the Sun'. All the stories are accompanied by Pohatu's vivid double-page illustrations. Paperback 215 x 230mm. PEN0349 Taming the Sun: Four MƒÅori Myths -√É‚Äö√Ç¬ÝFour classic MƒÅori myths beautifully retold within Taming the Sun with stunning illustrations by award winner Gavin Bishop. Taming the Sun contains four stories, including two well-known legends (Maui And The Sun and Maui And The Big Fish) and two less well-known legends (Rona And The Moon and Kahu The Taniwha). Aimed at childreges 3-7 years, these myths are simply and yet elegantly written. PEN0180 Matariki The MƒÅori New Year -√É‚Äö√Ç¬Ýis an introduction to the star group Matariki. Known in other cultures by names including the Pleiades and the Seven Sisters, Matariki featured strongly in pre-European New Zealand. It marked the beginning of the MƒÅori calendar, and its rising before the sun in late May or early June was greeted with great festivals. It was used as a guide to planting and harvesting, and was studied by tohunga as an omen which told whether the food-gathering season would be plentiful or lean. Libby Hakaraia includes interviews with astronomers Richard Hall and Vicki Hyde, well known navigator Hekenukumai Busby, and Hapimana Rikihana, who still practises the ancient MƒÅori art of mahi whai, or string patterns. Today more and more people are celebrating the rising of Matariki. For those who wish to join this growing movement, there are suggestions on how to celebrate this MƒÅori New Year - and, most ly, a guide to finding Matariki in the night sky. Paperback 200 x 231mm PEN4511 Illustrated MƒÅori Myths and Legends -√É‚Äö√Ç¬ÝQueenie Rikihana Hyland here brings together a collection of her favourite MƒÅori myths and legends - from the creation and the ever-popular mischief-making of Maui, to the great love stories of Hinemoa and Tutanekai, and Turongo and Mahinarangi. The stories are brought to life by the vivid illustrations of Patrick puru. Illustrated MƒÅori Myths and Legends is a stunning addition to any bookshelf, be that of a child or an adult. Experience it as part of a group being read to or read it alone - either way the stories and the illustrations will fascinate, interest and intrigue readers of all ages. Paperback 247 x 255mm. title: Nga Whetu Matariki Whanakotia
main description: Behind dusty orange hills, where the sky stretches down to the sea, there's a magical, wild, windy place called Te Mata Hapuku. Sam and Te Rerehua love to visit their Grandma and Poua at Te Mata Hapuku (aka Birdling's Flat). They like to listen to the stories, with a backdrop of whipping wind, flashing torchlight, and the splash of Poua's gaff in the water. But one night, Grandma notices something mysterious. Someone has stolen some stars from the sky. And then the patupaiarehe come out to play on the beach√¢‚Ç¨¬¶ Can Sam and Te Rerehua rescue the Matariki stars? A MƒÅori language edition. Nga Whetu Matariki Whanakotia = The Stolen Stars of Mat

title: Nga Wira o te Pahi (The Wheels on the Bus MƒÅori edition
main description:
The Wheels on the Bus MƒÅori Edition."Ko nga wira o te pahi ka taka-huri-huri . . ." E mohio whanuitia ana tenei waiata. Engari, inaianei kei te haere te pahi huri noa i to tatou whenua ataahua o Aotearoa nei, a, ka mohio pea koe ki etahi o nga kaieke: a Kiwi, a Hoiho, a Tuatara, a Piwakawaka, a wai atu, a wai atu. Ekea te pahi, waiatatia mai! He parekareka tenei pukapuka pikitia ma te kohungahunga na Donovan Bixley, koia nei tetahi o nga kaita pikitia e tino kaingakautia ana ki Aotearoa nei, ko etahi o ana pukapuka atu, ko TE PAMU O KORO MEKETANARA [OLD MACDONALD'S FARM] me te LOOKY BOOK, he mea tino hokohoko atu i Aotearoa nei. With the same gorgeous illustrations as the original book, the te reo MƒÅori translation retains the humour and vibrancy of the classic English-language edition. Translated by Ross Calman.

Donovan Bixley ONZM has sold hundreds of thousands of copies of his books in 31 countries and 18 languages, including te reo MƒÅori. He has won awards for both his illustrating and writing and won the Children's Choice award in 2013. In 2021 he became an Officer of the Order of N Zealand. With over 100 books published, his unique style of illustration brings delight and joy to readers both young and old. He lives on the crater's edge of the greatest volcanic eruption in human history (also known as Lake Taupo, in New Zealand). Music fills his spare time alongside his wife and three daughters.

title: He Raiona i roto i nga Otaota (A Lion in the Meadow MƒÅori edition
main description:
A Lion in the Meadow MƒÅori edition. I ki atu te tamaiti,"E whae, he raiona kei roto i nga otaota." Ka ki mai te whaea, "Horihori, e Tama." E mea ana koe he raiona kei roto i nga otaota? Ka takutakutia e Margaret Mahi tetahi paki whakamiharo, mo te pono, mo te pohewa. He maha nga tohu honore i whakawhiwhia ki a Margaret. He purakau tino whai take tenei ma ia tamaiti, ma ia tamaiti. He ataahua nga pikitia na Jenny Williams.

Margaret Mahy (1936-2012) is one of New Zealand's most celebrated children's writers. She is the author of more than 150 titles, which have been translated into many different languages and sold around the world. Appointed to the Order of New Zealand in 1993, Mahy also won many global prizes for children's writers, including the Carnegie Medal and the prestigious Hans Christian Andersen Award. 'It is in the nature of books, that they have the capacity to make you feel powerful about what you can alter and achieve in your life' - Margaret Mahy Jenny Williams (Illustrator) Jenny Williams is a hugely talented illustrator whose style is recognisable at a glance. A native of London, Williams was educated at the Wimbledon School of Art and University of London. She started drawing as a very young child, making up stories about the characters she created and her drawings have appeared in many different types of books, including fairy tales and nursery rhymes, animal stories and folk tales, published throughout the world.

title: Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star
main description: Who delivers mail to the birds of New Zealand? Join Ruru, the New Zealand native owl, while she delivers the mail to her avian friends like Tui and Piwakawaka at night . Discover which birds are nocturnal and track the Southern Cross constellation as it moves across the night sky. With Renee Chin's beautiful illustrations and the lyrics from Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star in both English and te reo MƒÅori this gorgeous picture book is a classic in the making. Renee Chin is a graduate of the Whanganui School of Design. Since 2009 she has been working as an illustrator in the advertising field during the day and creating hand-drawn art in her spare time. Her illustration is inspired by exploration, travel, animals and nature, places and things

title: Match It Kai Moana Set 12
main description: Match It Kai Moana was designed by Indigenous MƒÅori woman Kiri Pewhairangi from Aeotearoa. Kai Moana in Te reo MƒÅori means seafood. 12 mini cushions measuring 8 x 8cm are included which creates 6 matching pairs. Match the crayfish, clam, abalone, fish, basket and sea urchin. Included is a drawstring bag for storage. This product is made under Fair Trade conditions and designs may vary. Match It Kai Moana is carefully handmade by a group of artisans in Kolkata who specialize in the very intricate embroidery, under fair trade conditions in India. Fair Trade organisation was founded in Kolkata, West Bengal, India in 2003. Today over 150 local families are involved. The talented artisans hand make a diverse range of products, specialising in educational resources. This organisation provides the local workers with a sustainable income, uplifting them from poverty and providing hope for the future. All the ethics of fair trade are maintained. This organisation also provides emergency funds for the workers in timef need such as a medical emergency for the artisan or family members, funds are readily available so they can seek immediate assistance. They have also provide tutoring and school supplies for local children, they have built a school in Asansol, west of Kolkata.

title: Our County, Our World Big Book Set
main description:
Our County, Our World Big Book Set looks at the various ways children live in Australia and around the world. These big books are a great discussion starter for investigating Australia, the world, transport, cultures, counting, and natural environments. Clearly and simply written text is suitable for children of all ages.

Use these big books as a basis for questioning, further investigation or just to read, discuss and enjoy the photographs.
